speechOptions.instruction: Speak in a bright, warm, hopeful tone — like happily introducing something you love to a friend. Gentle, positive energy throughout; never dark or flat.

title: Life, Connected by Collections
description: Real examples of MulmoClaude collections: a kitchen week where recipes, the shopping list, and the ledger connect in one conversation, plus travel, study, and family uses — and how a collection grows smarter with use.

beats.text: MulmoClaude "collections". Personal mini-apps, born just by asking. Recipes, shopping lists, a household ledger. Apps that used to live apart now connect inside one AI — and grow the more you use them. Here are real examples, in three minutes.

beats.text: Until now, recipes lived in a recipe app, shopping lists in a memo, the ledger in a spreadsheet. Each was fine on its own — but connecting them was always your handiwork. In MulmoClaude, one AI holds all of your collections. So a single conversation connects everything.

beats.text: A collection is your own personal mini-app, born from a spoken request. It comes with friendly views — tables, calendars, galleries — and collections connect to each other through conversation. And it isn't finished at creation: it keeps growing as you talk.

They grow by talking
Say "make it so", and it changes on the spot. The more you use it, the more it's yours.
MulmoClaude · Collections in real life

beats.text: Let's watch it happen. One evening you ask: "Plan four dinners for this week. There's chicken and cabbage in the fridge". A recipe collection appears with the menu lined up — drawing on the family tastes you've shared and last week's records.

beats.text: Next, one more line: "make the shopping list for these". The four days of ingredients merge into one list, minus what you already have at home. At the store, open it on your phone and just tick things off.

beats.text: Back home, just snap the receipt and send it. It lands in the ledger collection, categorized and recorded. At month's end, ask "how's the grocery budget doing?" and you get the totals and a little review. From recipes to the ledger — one continuous conversation.

beats.text: The same thing works for travel. Talk over a destination, and an itinerary, a packing list, and a budget appear — a full travel kit, connected as one trip. And when you're back, your photos and notes can become a memory album.

beats.text: And there are as many uses as there are corners of life. Studying for an exam, the family schedule, books you've read, daily health. Each one becomes its own collection the moment you ask — and grows into your version of it.

MulmoClaude · Collections in real life

beats.text: And a collection grows the more you use it. In the first week, it simply does what you ask. A month in, it starts remembering your tastes and budget — and by month three, it's proposing first: "groceries are running high this month, shall we plan some thrifty dinners?"

beats.text: A collection isn't finished when it's made. It's something you raise in conversation, shaped to your life. Start with one — the thing you've always meant to organize — and ask MulmoClaude.
